title: When Judging Behavior: - for Others: Ask,
subtitle: Understand Context in Behavior
description:
When judging behavior:
- For others: Ask, "What situations might have influenced their actions?"
- For yourself: Reflect, "How much of this is about my choices versus circumstances?"
- Balance the view: Consider both personality and context for all parties.

Example: A colleague misses a deadline—consider if external factors, not just laziness, played a role.

faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

howTo.title: Identify a triggering interaction
howTo.summary: Choose one recent interaction that triggered a negative judgment. Reflect on the emotions it evoked and prepare to analyze it.

howTo.title: Pause and reflect
howTo.summary: Take 20 seconds to breathe and name the feeling. Ask yourself what situational factors might have influenced the other person's actions.

howTo.title: List situational and dispositional factors
howTo.summary: Write down two situational factors and one dispositional factor related to the interaction. This helps in understanding the context better.

howTo.title: Choose a constructive response
howTo.summary: Decide on one small action to take, such as asking a clarifying question or offering help, to foster a more positive interaction.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and insights. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/attribution-bias-coach/.
